title: On the local equivalence of trapped-ion two-qudit gates
authors: Nikita V. Semenin, Pavel A. Kamenskikh, Ilia V. Zalivako, Anastasiia S. Nikolaeva, Evgeniy O. Kiktenko
abstract: We derive a necessary condition of the local equivalence between two-qudit gates in terms of singular values of transformed gate matrices. This condition is valid for arbitrary qudit dimensions $d$ and is thus a relatively simple general way of checking whether two gates can be reduced to one another with single-qudit (local) gates. We use this condition to investigate the local equivalence of two widely used trapped-ion two-qubit gates in qudit space: the Molmer-Sorensen (MS) gate and a special case of the Light-Shift (LS) gate, both of which we studied in one of our previous works.
summary: This paper establishes a necessary condition for the local equivalence of two-qudit quantum gates by analyzing the singular values of their reshuffled matrices. Applying this to trapped-ion gates, the authors find that the widely used Mølmer-Sørensen and Light-Shift gates are only locally equivalent under specific conditions ($d=3, \chi=\pi$). This provides crucial theoretical constraints for implementing quantum gates in qudit systems.
main_problem: The paper derives a necessary condition to determine when two two-qudit quantum gates are locally equivalent, and applies this to compare the Mølmer-Sørensen (MS) and Light-Shift (LS) gates.
main_result: The MS and zero-order LS gates are only locally equivalent when the qudit dimension is $d=3$ and the nonlocal phase is $\chi=\pi$. For $d>3$, they are proven to be non-equivalent.
method: The analysis relies on comparing the singular value spectra (Schmidt coefficients) of the reshuffled gate matrices, $R(U)$, which provides a necessary condition for local equivalence.
model_or_system: The study concerns quantum gates acting on qudits within a trapped-ion system. It specifically compares the Mølmer-Sørensen (MS) gate and the zero-order Light-Shift (LS) gate.
key_observables: The operator Schmidt coefficients, $	ext{sv}(R(U))$, and the squared nonzero Schmidt coefficients ($\lambda^2_{LS}(\chi)$ and $\lambda^2_{MS}(\chi)$).
important_parameters: Qudit dimension ($d$), and the nonlocal phase ($\chi$).
main_assumptions: The derived condition based on Schmidt spectra is necessary but not sufficient for local equivalence. The analysis initially ignores local phases.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a schematic proof of the local equivalence theorem using tensor network notation, and Figure 2 illustrates the decomposition of the MS gate into the LS gate using local unitaries for the $d=3, \chi=\pi$ case.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the general necessary condition for local equivalence using singular values. It then applies this condition to the MS and LS gates, analyzing the general case ($d>2$) and the specific case ($\chi=\pi$) to derive the final equivalence criteria.
why_interesting: This work provides rigorous theoretical tools (Schmidt number analysis) to classify the fundamental equivalence of gates used in trapped-ion quantum computation, directly impacting the design and understanding of quantum gates in AMO platforms.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic proof of the Theorem on the local equivalence of two-qudit gates using tensor network notation. QX and PX denote the left and right unitary matrices obtained from the singular value decomposition of the matrix X.
Figure 2: Decomposition of UMS(π) into U (0) LS (π) using local unitaries for d = 3
